DAILY PROMPT
Tuesday's craft suggestion

Stitch a small pocket of joy — try a four-petal daisy in your favourite yellow thread. Share it with #PocketDaisy.

Today · Tuesday, 14 May
Eleanor Whitfield
Hand quilter · Two hours ago

Finally finished the appliqué binding on my mother's old quilt block. Forty years it sat in a drawer — today it caught the morning light. There's something tender about putting fresh thread through old cotton.

HAND QUILTING
Applaud · 84
Comment · 12
Save
Harold Beaumont
Wood turner · Four hours ago

A little walnut bud-vase off the lathe this morning. The grain on this one made me stop and smile three times. Took two evenings of careful sanding — patience is the real tool.

WOOD TURNING
Applaud · 56
Comment · 9
Save
Beatrice Holloway
Botanical embroidery · This afternoon

Sketching this autumn leaf to start a new embroidery hoop next week. I'll work it in long-and-short stitch with three shades of russet. Anyone have a favourite linen weight for botanicals?

EMBROIDERY
Applaud · 41
Comment · 18
Save
Yesterday · Monday, 13 May
Frances Tilbury
Wheel-thrown pottery · Yesterday at 5:42 pm

Pulled this little planter out of the kiln yesterday — first piece since my elbow surgery in March. Slow work, but the wheel feels like an old friend who waited patiently for me. Already filled it with garden roses.

POTTERY
Applaud · 132
Comment · 27
Save
Ronald Ashcombe
Bookbinding · Yesterday at 11:08 am

Coptic-stitched a fresh travel journal for my granddaughter's gap year. Linen thread, cover marbled by hand last spring. She wrote her first entry over morning tea — best payment a binder could ask for.

BOOKBINDING
Applaud · 98
Comment · 15
Save
Earlier this week
Constance Murray
Knitwear · Sunday at 3:20 pm

Cast on a pink mohair shawl for spring — soft as a whisper and twice as warm. Pattern is the Tilden Wrap from page 42 of last month's club booklet. Pop your row counters on, ladies!

KNITTING
Applaud · 67
Comment · 11
Save
Albert Henley
Paper craft · Saturday at 9:15 am

Pressed leaves from last autumn finally got their home — a paper-marbled keepsake card for my sister's 80th. There's something about layered paper that feels like layered memory. Recipe in the comments.

PAPER CRAFT

Comment · 21
